prompt: [Inventory Turnover] = COGS over the period / AVERAGE inventory value during the period. COGS = units sold * Product[Standard Cost]. Average inventory value = average across the days of the daily total (Units On Hand * Standard Cost). Retail-v2.

assertions.text: COGS = units sold valued at Product[Standard Cost], e.g. SUMX('Sales', 'Sales'[Units] * RELATED('Product'[Standard Cost]))
Average inventory is computed by AVERAGING over the days in the period (AVERAGEX over VALUES('Date'[Date])), i.e. semi-additive averaged over time, NOT summed across days
Inventory is valued at Standard Cost (Units On Hand * 'Product'[Standard Cost]) inside the daily aggregation
Uses DIVIDE for the turnover ratio
Defines multiple VARs (COGS, average inventory) and references only existing columns
